[2018-03-17 07:15] danf_eldenlord (DanF): Any philosophy that says higher cost units should always be worth more than lower cost units in every state is not a viable philosophy
[2018-03-17 07:16] danf_eldenlord (DanF): The opportunity to gain excess value with lower cost units represents gameplay, not a problem
[2018-03-17 07:17] danf_eldenlord (DanF): The basic rule "having a unit is always better than not having that unit, all else being equal" is reasonable to follow and has benefits for AI programming.
[2018-03-17 07:21] http404error: I don't see what your first two sentences are in response to
[2018-03-17 07:21] http404error: Oh, I'm not saying Steelsplitter should dominate Wall because of its cost at all
[2018-03-17 07:21] http404error: They just happen to be directly comparable in terms of health and abilities, except for the ability to attack
[2018-03-17 07:22] http404error: In the example I gave that's a nonfactor
[2018-03-17 07:22] danf_eldenlord (DanF): I'd argue that cost is a better "strictly better factor" than basically any other stat
[2018-03-17 07:22] http404error: Anyways, it's true that units have an innate quality that gives them incomparable value - their name
[2018-03-17 07:23] http404error: So no unit ever needs to be strictly better than another unit
[2018-03-17 07:23] http404error: There are times you'd rather have an Engineer than Lucina after all
[2018-03-17 07:23] danf_eldenlord (DanF): and since even higher cost units shouldn't always be strictly better than lower cost units...
[2018-03-17 07:23] danf_eldenlord (DanF): That means any of the fuzzier comparisons are grandfathered in as well
[2018-03-17 07:24] http404error: Sorry, which fuzzier comparisons?
[2018-03-17 07:24] danf_eldenlord (DanF): any argument that is less clear than higher/lower cost
[2018-03-17 07:24] danf_eldenlord (DanF): being a lower cost than another unit is basically as close as you can get to strictly better
[2018-03-17 07:25] http404error: That seems simplistic
[2018-03-17 07:25] http404error: "Start of turn: add X" is a strictly good ability to have
[2018-03-17 07:26] http404error: there are tons of strictly good things like that
[2018-03-17 07:26] danf_eldenlord (DanF): if it's alright for even lower cost units to occasionally be better than higher cost units without violating the goals intended in SDR, when the entire game is based around higher cost stuff being more valuable in general than lower cost stuff...
[2018-03-17 07:26] danf_eldenlord (DanF): then the fuzzier comparisons are grandfathered in as well
[2018-03-17 07:26] danf_eldenlord (DanF): after all, resources have high utility as they can be spent on whatever is most valuable at the moment

[2018-03-17 07:51] kaelii: Because that is basically a drone plus blastforge for a drone's cost.

[2018-03-17 07:53] http404error: and just get one of these instead

[2018-03-17 07:54] aeich (H): would you rather change its cost (maybe add a red) or make its effect weaker (ex no more gain a gold)

[2018-03-17 07:55] f300xen (f0x): it's really hard to balance

Gain 1 :Attack: for every Animus owned

[2018-03-17 07:57] kaelii: I mean, they're already FINE on 1x animus 1x Blast Forge.
[2018-03-17 07:57] vanitascabal6962 (VanitasCabal): Yeah I originally thought 1 supply
[2018-03-17 07:57] vanitascabal6962 (VanitasCabal): Maybe increase the cost and 4 supply is more interesting tho
[2018-03-17 08:04] mqp: to be clear they are totally amazing on 1x animus
[2018-03-17 08:05] mqp: close to the most efficient attacker in the game
[2018-03-17 08:05] mqp: i think every game involving this will result in 4 of these and 4 animuses
[2018-03-17 08:05] mqp: i honestly don't think there is a cost that would make this both playable in normal games and not busted in long games, it's like a kind of amporilla that doesn't have the difficult up-front investment before you profit
[2018-03-17 08:07] aeich (H): Antima Drone (8 :Gold: :Energy: :Energy: ,  4 supply), 3 :Fragile: 
Start of turn: gain :Gold: :Green: :Blue: :Red: :Energy: 

On turn end each player gains all :Blue: :Red: :Energy: that is lost by their opponent

Changed the end of turn effect and adjusted the cost, what do you think?
[2018-03-17 08:10] http404error: the symmetrical effect is definitely interesting but it is very very difficult to reason about simply because it's so zany
[2018-03-17 08:10] danf_eldenlord (DanF): The non-symmetrical version is better
[2018-03-17 08:11] danf_eldenlord (DanF): Giving you abundant resources on the condition that you must find a way to spend all of them is cool
[2018-03-17 08:12] danf_eldenlord (DanF): punishing the opponent for waste as well, espescially when you're giving them stuff they might not otherwise want and thus causing them to waste, is less so
[2018-03-17 08:12] aeich (H): Yeah I guess there's just to much that could be going on compared to the non symmetrical one
[2018-03-17 08:12] f300xen (f0x): yeah but it's probably too easy to spend them
[2018-03-17 08:13] danf_eldenlord (DanF): Tuning might be off, I wouldn't know, but the core design is very interesting in the non-symmetrical version
[2018-03-17 08:13] danf_eldenlord (DanF): just need to be sure the difficulty in using all the resources is significant
[2018-03-17 08:13] danf_eldenlord (DanF): I'd make this change
[2018-03-17 08:14] danf_eldenlord (DanF): "At the end of your turn, your opponent gains :Gold: for each of your wasted :Red: :Blue: :Energy:
[2018-03-17 08:16] aeich (H): Your version is a bigger downside right?
[2018-03-17 08:16] http404error: Possibly more brutal! But it definitely compartmentalizes the decision of how to spend, which is good.
[2018-03-17 08:16] http404error: not knowing what tech you'll have at the start of your turn is... weird
[2018-03-17 08:17] aeich (H): Yeah I guess that's a good change
[2018-03-17 08:23] aeich (H): Antima Drone (3 :Gold: :Energy: :Energy: :Energy: ,  4 supply), 3 :Fragile: 
Start of turn: gain :Gold: :Green: :Blue: :Red: :Energy: 

At the end of your turn, your opponent gains Gold for each of your wasted :Red: :Blue: :Energy:

I also changed its cost to 3 energy, I feel like this could solve its problem in making aggro to strong
[2018-03-17 08:27] aeich (H): In this version, because you are generating more energy than you can use, your opponent also won't want to kill your engis
[2018-03-17 08:34] http404error: In practice they will still want to kill your engis

[2018-03-17 08:43] danf_eldenlord (DanF): I like cutting the energy cost for simplicity

[2018-03-17 19:35] .bky_1556 (bky): How much does Leviathan cost up front? Let's say 6 for an otherwise-worthless Animus, followed by 12RRRR.
[2018-03-17 19:35] .bky_1556 (bky): so (counting opportunity cost on the 6 gold) about 20RRRR total.
[2018-03-17 19:36] .bky_1556 (bky): How much is 5 attack worth? About 24RRRR?
[2018-03-17 19:36] .bky_1556 (bky): So, say you're using Ferratin Sacs.
[2018-03-17 19:37] .bky_1556 (bky): Further suppose you are also spending RRR on Tarsiers or something.
[2018-03-17 19:37] .bky_1556 (bky): The turn after you buy Leviathan, you spend 3 gold to negate the remaining RRR.
[2018-03-17 19:38] .bky_1556 (bky): The turn after that you spend 3 more gold on Ferratins.
[2018-03-17 19:39] .bky_1556 (bky): On subsequent turns, until you run out of Ferratin Sacs in 6 turns, you click the Ferratins to supply the gold to buy more Ferratins.
[2018-03-17 19:39] .bky_1556 (bky): That leaves you with an effective cost just slightly over the 24RRRR, after accounting for inflation.
[2018-03-17 19:40] .bky_1556 (bky): Nitrocybes require a steady gold input; Nitrocybes in don't directly pay for more Nitrocybes.
[2018-03-17 19:41] .bky_1556 (bky): But the 1 defense or damage is usually worth more than 1 gold.
[2018-03-17 19:41] .bky_1556 (bky): So Leviathan+Nitrocybe ends up worthwhile.
[2018-03-17 19:43] .bky_1556 (bky): Husks are more expensive. But if you don't care about the R cost, and you need the prompt defense, they're actually pretty close to Wall efficiency.
[2018-03-17 19:44] .bky_1556 (bky): The premium you're paying to get rid of R works out to a fraction of a gold per turn.
[2018-03-17 19:46] .bky_1556 (bky): If you buy a drone together with your Leviathan and buy husks instead of wall-efficiency soak, and waste the B you'd spend on soak walls, you pay 23RRRRE.
[2018-03-17 19:47] .bky_1556 (bky): Of course, if you buy it off Fuge and either Flame Animus or Chrono Filter, you can get it while only having a single Animus.
[2018-03-17 19:48] .bky_1556 (bky): At that point, you might well be able to keep it alive for a while with just Tarsier/Rhino.

messages.content: The turn after that you spend 3 more gold on Ferratins.

messages.content: On subsequent turns, until you run out of Ferratin Sacs in 6 turns, you click the Ferratins to supply the gold to buy more Ferratins.

messages.author: .bky_1556
messages.content: That leaves you with an effective cost just slightly over the 24RRRR, after accounting for inflation.

messages.author: .bky_1556

messages.author: .bky_1556
messages.content: The premium you're paying to get rid of R works out to a fraction of a gold per turn.

messages.author: .bky_1556
messages.content: If you buy a drone together with your Leviathan and buy husks instead of wall-efficiency soak, and waste the B you'd spend on soak walls, you pay 23RRRRE.

[2018-03-18 02:37] supergreyman (Greyman): The only thing I disagree is that the Mind Control Tech shouldn't introduce an RNG ability.
[2018-03-18 02:37] .jrkirby (jrkirby): Off by 1 event idea: 

At the start, all units have a 33% chance to cost 1 extra gold, and a 33% chance to cost 1 less gold if they don't cost 0 gold.

When are units units are strong even when they cost extra? When does a small price decrease push something over the line? How does the game change with 4 gold drones and 1 gold engineers?
[2018-03-18 02:42] supergreyman (Greyman): I can imagine that things that are already low price would grow stronger if they cost 1 gold less than those that are higher priced. For example, Auric Impulse with -1 gold would have -33% lower cost, compared to Odin which would only incur -5% lower cost.
[2018-03-18 02:42] supergreyman (Greyman): An increase cost of +1 gold would have the opposite effect.
[2018-03-18 02:43] f300xen (f0x): that seems like it would be incredibly player favored
[2018-03-18 02:44] supergreyman (Greyman): Things like increased cost of Auric Impulse would make it literally useless.
[2018-03-18 02:45] supergreyman (Greyman): Because anything with low cost already has a very tight design.
[2018-03-18 02:46] .jrkirby (jrkirby): It's meant to shake things up to add interest for an event, not be a super competitive mode
[2018-03-18 02:48] supergreyman (Greyman): If it's an event, then yeah, game balance means nothing. :D
Just theorycrafting how much it would change if that were to happen.

[2018-03-18 04:42] .bky_1556 (bky): Bubble Screen (3G + Drone, supply 4) 4 Health
Fragile, Blocker, Stamina 3
Click: Gain 1 gold and 1 health
[2018-03-18 04:43] .bky_1556 (bky): It's a slow Plexo without the lifespan drawback
[2018-03-18 04:43] .bky_1556 (bky): OR you can absorb on it, then have the drone inside keep producing for a few turns while rebuilding towards a final soak.
[2018-03-18 04:46] .bky_1556 (bky): OR you can buy it several turns before a predictable burst (e.g. Wincer) and click a few times to rack up soak potential.
[2018-03-18 04:47] .bky_1556 (bky): (aside from "this is miscosted")
[2018-03-18 04:50] .jrkirby (jrkirby): That's actually really cool
[2018-03-18 04:50] .jrkirby (jrkirby): It's really non-trivial to figure out which turn to absorb on it
[2018-03-18 04:59] .bky_1556 (bky): It's for the challenge, of course.
[2018-03-18 05:04] http404error: It would work well with lifespan also but I think that can lead to more feelbad, so the stamina works well here
[2018-03-18 05:05] http404error: It seems strong to me
[2018-03-18 05:05] http404error: Also I would have pegged it as a supply 10 unit
[2018-03-18 05:12] .bky_1556 (bky): Noted on the 10 supply, that's the part I thought least about.
[2018-03-18 05:13] http404error: It is pretty complicated to track in multiples since it has two variables
[2018-03-18 05:13] .bky_1556 (bky): For reference, Sentinel.
[2018-03-18 05:13] http404error: Yeah, Sentinel's variables don't affect each other though
[2018-03-18 05:13] http404error: Besides like, don't ditch your 3 stamina 1hp sentinel
[2018-03-18 05:15] .bky_1556 (bky): I think it would be the only 10 supply 6+ HP unit, even if it takes a while to get there.
[2018-03-18 05:16] .bky_1556 (bky): Is fragile+lifespan a forbidden combo?
[2018-03-18 05:21] .bky_1556 (bky): Anyway, in addition to the feelbad, lifespan would present a balance issue
[2018-03-18 05:21] .bky_1556 (bky): namely, it disadvantages absorbing with it too much over clicking up and soaking
[2018-03-18 05:22] .bky_1556 (bky): since the absorb turn forgoes a click, permanently.
[2018-03-18 05:22] .bky_1556 (bky): It also wouldn't mesh well with the patient defense theme of the unit to suddenly need to BLOCK NOW
[2018-03-18 05:23] .bky_1556 (bky): as opposed to running out of rubidium and blocking by inertia.
[2018-03-18 05:25] .bky_1556 (bky): aside - the non-prompt soak niche in mono-green is really empty. The main options are "buy engineer" and "buy drones while storing G".
[2018-03-18 05:27] http404error: Patient defense in green is definitely a novel thing.
